questions.question: Assertion (A): If a line divides any two sides of a triangle in the same ratio, then the line is parallel to the third side. Reason (R): This is the Converse of Basic Proportionality Theorem.

steps: Identify the statement as the definition of the converse of Thales Theorem (BPT).
Confirm that the reason correctly identifies the theorem name.
solution.derivation: A: \text{Converse of BPT holds true.} \ R: \text{Definition of Converse of BPT.}

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.question: Assertion (A): Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal. Reason (R): If two triangles are equiangular, they are similar by AAA similarity criterion.

steps: Check if equality of angles leads to similarity.
solution.derivation: A: \text{Angle-Angle-Angle Similarity Criterion} \ R: \text{AAA creates proportional sides.}

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.question: Assertion (A): In $\triangle ABC$, if $DE \parallel BC$ such that $AD/DB = 1/2$ and $AE = 2 \text{ cm}$, then $EC = 4 \text{ cm}$. Reason (R): By Basic Proportionality Theorem, if $DE \parallel BC$, then $AD/DB = AE/EC$.
options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.question: Assertion (A): All congruent triangles are similar. Reason (R): All similar triangles are congruent.

steps: Congruent triangles have same shape and size, so they are similar.
Similar triangles have same shape but not necessarily same size, so they are not always congruent.

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.question: Assertion (A): The ratio of the areas of two similar triangles is equal to the square of the ratio of their corresponding sides. Reason (R): This is the Area of Similar Triangles Theorem.

steps: Recall the theorem regarding areas of similar triangles.
Confirm that the statement matches the standard theorem.

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
